data.content: This project uses `packages.config` format. I'll migrate it to `PackageReference` and then set up Central Package Management (CPM).
data.reasoningText:
This project uses `packages.config` format, which is the old-style NuGet package management. To convert to Central Package Management (CPM), I first need to convert from `packages.config` to `PackageReference` format, and then set up CPM with a `Directory.Packages.props` file.

Let me proceed:

1. Convert the `.csproj` from the old format with `packages.config` to SDK-style (or at least to PackageReference format)
2. Create a `Directory.Packages.props` with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` set to true
3. Remove the `packages.config` file


4. I see the `convert-to-cpm` skill is available, but it explicitly can't handle `packages.config` projects—I need to migrate to PackageReference first. So I'll manually convert the project file to use PackageReference instead of the old Reference+HintPath pattern, remove the packages.config file, and then create the Directory.Packages.props to centralize versions.

I'm considering whether to convert to SDK-style format as well, but the user only asked for CPM conversion, so I'll stick with that. The old-style project format can still use PackageReference and work with CPM, so I'll just swap out the Reference elements for PackageReference entries and set up the centralized version management.

diff --git a/tmp/sv-07d85447dc8d44038e76d803afbb6d5b/simple-packages-config/Directory.Packages.props b/tmp/sv-07d85447dc8d44038e76d803afbb6d5b/simple-packages-config/Directory.Packages.props
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-07d85447dc8d44038e76d803afbb6d5b/simple-packages-config/Directory.Packages.props
@@ -1,0 +1,9 @@
+<Project>
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

data.content:
Done. Here's what I changed:

1. **`LegacyApp.csproj`** — Replaced the old `<Reference>` with HintPath with a `<PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />` (no version, since CPM manages it).
2. **`Directory.Packages.props`** (new) — Created at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and `Newtonsoft.Json` version `13.0.3` centralized.
3. **`packages.config`** — Deleted.
